mission.title: istio: Better support for sidecar containers in batch jobs
mission.description: Better support for sidecar containers in batch jobs. This issue affects 76+ users.

steps.title: Identify istio feature symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your istio deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=istio
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=istio -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors related to: Better support for sidecar containers in batch jobs

steps.title: Check current Job configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant istio resources:
```bash
kubectl get job -A
kubectl describe job <name> -n <namespace>
```
Sidecar containers do not play well with k8s jobs. The job will keep running so long as the sidecar proxy is running. This is a general problem not specific to Istio (see https://github.com/kubernetes

steps.title: Apply the fix for Better support for sidecar containers in batch jobs
steps.description:
It fixes #6324 

I looked through both envoy and pilot-agent and could notice [Agent waits until the next 'config update'](https://github.com/istio/istio/blob/a4df8f85dcf3c32d4fd944e69f662a51b1eb43da/pilot/pkg/proxy/agent.go#L185), if the last epoch terminates normally. I think in case of 'config up
```yaml
State:          Terminated
      Reason:       Error
      Exit Code:    143
```

steps.title: Confirm Better support for sidecar containers in batch job is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=istio -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
The root cause is: It fixes #6324 

I looked through both envoy and pilot-agent and could notice [Agent waits until the next 'config update'](https://github.com/istio/istio/blob/a4df8f85dcf3c32d4fd944e69f662a51b1eb43da/pilot/pkg/proxy/agent.go#L185), if the last epoch terminates normally. I think in case of 'config update' Istio users do not explicitly terminate epochs, since it supports hot-reloading. So the main p. This fixes the issue because it addresses the underlying problem that was causing the failure.

      Reason:       Error

Command:
     sh
     -c
     sleep 20;/usr/bin/curl http://dl-cdn.alpinelinux.org --fail; exit_code=$?; echo code is $exit_code; pkill -f /usr/local/bin/pilot-agent; exit $exit_code;
   State:          Terminated

Command:
      sh
      -c
      sleep 20;/usr/bin/curl http://dl-cdn.alpinelinux.org --fail; exit_code=$?; echo code is $exit_code; pkill -INT /usr/local/bin/pilot-agent; exit $exit_code;
    State:          Terminated
      Reason:       Completed
      Exit Code:    0

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with istio installed or the issue environment reproducible.
